water.name: Marlette Lake

authorities.name: U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Marlette Lake, tributaries and outlet stream: Season: July 15 through September 30. Limits — Daily: 0 trout, catch and release only. Hours: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

sources.title: Marlette Lake — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Marlette Lake covers 381 surface acres with a maximum depth of 45 feet. Located in Washoe County... Species present: Rainbow trout (12-18 inches), Cutthroat trout (12-20 inches), Brook trout (10-18 inches). Marlette Lake is closed to vehicles and anglers must walk, mountain bike, or ride horseback about 5 miles from Spooner Lake. Stocking Updates: 1754 Cutthroat Trout, 8.1 in, 09-05-2023; 5058 Rainbow Trout, 9.6 in, 07-31-2023. Pertinent Information: the lake is used as a brood lake for rainbow and cutthroat trout where spawning operations occur annually and offspring are used to stock waterbodies around the state. Brook trout were introduced in the 1880's, Lahontan cutthroat trout in 1964, and Tahoe strain rainbow trout in 1984.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

parameters.note: Marlette Lake is a Lahontan cutthroat and rainbow trout broodstock/egg-collection lake for NDOW; it is only open to fishing July 15 through September 30, and only on a catch-and-release basis. Access is on foot, mountain bike, or horseback (about 5 miles from Spooner Lake); confirm trail/park access status before a trip.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Washoe County, Marlette Lake
regulations.humanSummary: Marlette Lake, its tributaries, and outlet stream are open to fishing only July 15 through September 30, catch-and-release only.

parameters.counting_rule: All trout (rainbow, Lahontan cutthroat, and brook trout) must be released; none may be kept.

parameters.note: 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset.

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is permitted from 1 hour before sunrise to 2 hours after sunset at Marlette Lake.

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
